title: leucanthemum aragonense
intro: Leucanthemum aragonense, also known as Aragonese Oxeye Daisy, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Leucanthemum aragonense include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve overall well-being, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
